issues.description: Stylistic form −x^(−2) = −1/x^2 was added (good), but the parenthetical 'holds for all real n' from the prior review fix was not picked up. This is genuinely minor — AP rubrics accept either statement of the rule and most textbooks defer the proof for irrational n — but it would be a one-token add if a revision pass is happening anyway.
issues.fix: Optionally append '(holds for all real n)' next to the power rule banner.

overall_comment: All previously flagged items were addressed. The dy/dx framing on slide 06 is now mathematically honest and pedagogically forward-compatible with separation of variables. The point-form limit definition and the tangent-line equation y = f(a) + f'(a)(x − a) are present on slide 05 and echoed in the recap. Slide 07 now names the symmetric difference quotient, explains why it is more accurate (averaging forward and backward secants), and gives the units rule. Slide 08 lists all four differentiability failure modes (corner, cusp, vertical tangent, discontinuity) with concrete examples. The quotient-rule worked example on slide 14 is simplified to (x cos x − sin x)/x^2. The trig pattern note on slide 16 correctly identifies cosine, cotangent, and cosecant as the minus-sign carriers and explicitly excludes secant. Slide 13 (pause1_silence) now labels f, f', g, g' in a box mirroring slide 11's product-rule template. All formulas re-verified: power, product, quotient, all six trig derivatives, e^x, and ln x (with the x > 0 domain note now present). The sole remaining 'minor' is the stylistic 'all real n' parenthetical on the power rule, which is not blocking. Verdict: pass.
